title: ‘Civil War’ With Alex Garland! Plus: The 10 Most Anticipated Movies Out of CinemaCon.
format: Sean reports back from CinemaCon with the slate that got the loudest room, then he, Amanda, and Chris Ryan spoiler-talk Alex Garland's Civil War, and Sean sits down with Garland himself.

segments.heading: The CinemaCon Report
segments.note: the slate Sean saw in Vegas

films.title: Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga
films.note: George Miller showed an eight-minute teaser and said he'd locked the movie days earlier. Sean had been worried by the trailer; the extended footage flipped him: a 20-year saga rather than Fury Road's three-day chase.

films.title: Mickey 17
films.note: Bong Joon-ho's sci-fi with Robert Pattinson doing 'a real choice on the voice,' plus Steven Yeun and Mark Ruffalo. Sean read the January date as a head-scratcher: closer to Okja's zaniness than Parasite or Snowpiercer.

films.title: Nosferatu
films.note: Robert Eggers being sold as commercial, 'Dracula's back and loving it.' The trailer withholds Bill Skarsgård but has seven or eight characters say 'he's coming.' Sean, admittedly in the bag for Eggers, called it the best-looking thing he saw.

films.title: MaXXXine
films.note: Ty West's third Mia Goth movie; A24 wasn't at CinemaCon but the trailer ran parallel. Michelle Monaghan plays a cop, 'not since Gone Baby Gone.'

films.title: Beetlejuice Beetlejuice
films.note: The noisiest room at CinemaCon, whole cast plus Tim Burton present. All practical effects, no CGI. Sean is skeptical Burton's made a good movie in 30 years but loved the 1984-1995 run.

films.title: Joker: Folie à Deux
films.note: Todd Phillips told the room he and Joaquin couldn't turn down another $500 million; elided whether it's a musical, saying music was 'essential' though the trailer shows no singing.

films.title: Horizon: An American Saga - Chapter 1
films.note: Kevin Costner showed an extended cut of part one and, per Sean, 'red-stated, blue-stated' his remarks to exhibitors. Sean is cold on another eight-hour two-part Western but can't wait to watch Costner sell it in public.

films.title: Megalopolis
films.note: The talk of Vegas: Mike Fleming's 'bold and audacious' piece, then Kim Masters relaying studio chiefs walking out. Every studio person Sean spoke to said no way; something 'experiential' Coppola tried reportedly didn't land.

films.title: Good Fortune
films.note: Sean's surprise of the show: Aziz Ansari's directorial feature, dropped by one studio and picked up by Lionsgate, with Keanu Reeves as the angel Gabriel, Seth Rogen, and Ansari. Ansari killed as a live presenter, 'the first actually good live entertainer' in four days.

films.title: Twisters
films.note: Lee Isaac Chung's storm movie, 'looks like it rocks,' not much more to say.

films.title: Ballerina
films.note: The John Wick spinoff pushed to 2025 after Chad Stahelski came in and ordered five new action sequences. Set between John Wick 3 and 4; Keanu in the trailer.

films.title: Wicked
films.note: A long extended clip and a full light show. Sean finds the fervor 'deeply puzzling': he thought the movie looked bad even as the room treated it like the second coming, discussed in the same breath as Beetlejuice for owning Thanksgiving.

films.title: Moana 2
films.note: Sean skipped the Disney presentation but flagged it against Wicked in November; reportedly no Lin-Manuel Miranda songs, and a live-action Moana with the Rock got pushed to 2026.

films.title: Mufasa: The Lion King
films.note: Cited as evidence the Disney live-action machine 'isn't really working anymore.'

films.title: Trap
films.note: Sean's favorite trailer of the show. M. Night Shyamalan's concert thriller with Josh Hartnett: a dad takes his daughter to a pop concert that turns out to be a police trap for a serial killer, who is revealed to be him. Sean thought the trailer gave away too much and loved it anyway.

films.title: Speak No Evil
films.note: The English-language remake of the 2022 Danish horror Sean and Chris loved, now with James McAvoy, Scoot McNairy, and Mackenzie Davis. Sean thinks the trailer spoiled 'every single thing.'

films.title: Conclave
films.note: Edward Berger's papal-election thriller, with Ralph Fiennes, Stanley Tucci, and John Lithgow. 'Ornately costumed,' prestige-y, entirely inside the conclave walls. Amanda once had a papal-conclave blog.

films.title: Michael
films.note: The Michael Jackson biopic from producer Graham King (Bohemian Rhapsody, Argo), directed by Antoine Fuqua, written by John Logan. Jaafar Jackson stars, Colman Domingo as Joe Jackson, Nia Long as his mother, Miles Teller as the lawyer. Sean got the sense everyone in the room was relieved it'll make a billion dollars, 'which made me just ill.'

films.title: Springsteen: Deliver Me from Nowhere
films.note: Scott Cooper's Bruce Springsteen movie about the making of Nebraska, with Jeremy Allen White. Sean thinks White is a good Bruce and will 'stand in the theater saluting' rather than sit; framed as the artier, riskier counterpart to Michael.

films.title: Superman
films.note: James Gunn's reboot with David Corenswet and Rachel Brosnahan; no footage shown. Sean's only note: Corenswet wore an Eagles sweatshirt at the presentation, 'if Superman wears an Eagle sweatshirt, I'll see it 500 times.'

films.title: Deadpool & Wolverine
films.note: Paramount and Disney showed 20 minutes on the day Sean left early; he chose to come home rather than spoil it, wanting to experience it pure.

films.title: Oh, Canada
films.note: Paul Schrader's new film with Jacob Elordi and Richard Gere. The Elordi casting is what suddenly got Sean interested.

films.title: Kinds of Kindness
films.note: Yorgos Lanthimos's anthology, out in June after Poor Things. On the teaser alone Sean said 'Yorgos is back': people doing awful things to each other for two hours, more his Dogtooth register than Poor Things.

films.title: The Apprentice
films.note: Ali Abbasi's (Holy Spider) Trump origin film set in the 1980s, with Sebastian Stan as Trump and Jeremy Strong as Roy Cohn. Sean wonders whether Trump is 'beyond cinematic depiction at this point.'

films.title: The Shrouds
films.note: A new Cronenberg, 'always exciting for me.'

segments.heading: ‘Civil War’
segments.note: with Chris Ryan and Bobby Wagner, full spoilers

films.title: Civil War
films.note: Alex Garland's $50M A24 near-future war movie, with Kirsten Dunst, Wagner Moura, Cailee Spaeny, Stephen McKinley Henderson, Jesse Plemons, and Nick Offerman, following journalists across a collapsed America. Chris calls it his favorite film of the year and 'the zenith of A24-core' filmmaking. Amanda: an amazing experience 'with basically no characters or ideas,' effective but maybe empty. The room argues whether an anti-war movie can survive its own thrills (the Plemons scene, the De La Soul shootout, the White House raid) and whether the Texas-California alliance and the withheld politics are provocation or evasion. Bobby's line: 'intellectually dumb and cinematically smart.'

films.title: Civil War
films.note: Garland says the movie came out of anger more than anxiety, about polarization and about journalism losing trust as institutions became 'propagandists more than journalists.' He calls himself a centrist, defends withholding 'flags' (the Texas-California alliance is an inference, not a hole), and explains the De La Soul needle-drop as the assertion that keeps it from inadvertently being a pro-war movie.

films.title: Ex Machina
films.note: Garland's debut, which the hosts and he both return to: a small, talky, cerebral movie with a 'bewitching' visual-effects heart. Sean argues it's a more successful film than Civil War.

films.title: Annihilation
films.note: Cited as part of Garland's episodic method: days inside the Shimmer, one transfigured encounter after another. Amanda's least favorite of his.

films.title: Men
films.note: His previous film, the confined, mythic one; the group can't agree what its birthing-cycle ending means, which becomes the template for the 'is that it?' argument about Civil War.

films.title: 28 Days Later
films.note: Reached for as the earliest example of Garland's structure: a series of encounters, each one dispensing a little more information.

referenced.group: Touchstones in the Civil War talk
